text.title: External Secrets Operator Recipe Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.description: ClusterSecretStore uses Vault static root token; token leaks via kubectl get secret.
antipattern.reason: Quick-start setup used static token; never rotated to identity auth.
antipattern.note: Fix: Switch to Vault Kubernetes auth method. Rotate the root token. Audit all ClusterSecretStore manifests.

antipattern.description: Backend rate-limits ESO; sync failures appear in logs; pods get stale secrets.
antipattern.note: Fix: Set refreshInterval to 1h default; <5min only for dynamic creds with explicit rate-limit budget.

antipattern.description: Any pod in cluster can read any secret via ESO; security review flags it.
antipattern.reason: ClusterExternalSecret used by default; no namespace scope.
antipattern.note: Fix: Convert to namespaced ExternalSecret. Use ClusterExternalSecret only with named approver + justification.

antipattern.description: Database creds rotated 3 days ago; app still using old creds; sync failed silently.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add PrometheusRule: alert on increase(eso_externalsecret_sync_calls_error_total[5m]) > 0. Page on-call.

antipattern.description: Crash dump uploaded to S3 contains plain-text API token in env block.
antipattern.note: Fix: Switch to file mount under /var/run/secrets/. App reads file on startup or SIGHUP. Audit for other env-var leaks.
